calendar.matters:
Notice Of Hearing On Petition To Compel Arbitration
Off Calendar May-30-2019 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
Gary Provencher, Esq. 916-443-6600 of Weinberg, Roger & Rosenfeld for Petitioner through Court Call.
William Quinn Jr., Esq. 415-241-6054 of San Francisco Public Schools for Respondent San Francisco Unified School District.

RULING - A R G U E D. The Court inquires why the express statement is not dispositive. Respondent argues. Petitioner responds. (as fully reported in the reporter's notes) The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

PETITIONER INTERNATIONAL UNION OF OPERATING ENGINEERS' Petition To Compel Arbitration.

The Court adopts the tentative ruling.Petitioner International Union of Operating Engineers, Stationary Engineers Local 39, AFL-CIO's petition to compel arbitration is granted. The collective bargaining agreement ("CBA") does not include a clear and unmistakable delegation clause. Therefore, the court rather than the arbitrator will determine whether petitioner "waived" the right to arbitrate by failing to seek arbitration in compliance with the timing requirements of the CBA. (See Code Civ. Proc. 1281.2(a) [court determines waiver]; Platt Pacific Inc. v. Andelson (1993) 6 Cal.4th 307, 313-314 [court determines whether parties' condition precedent regarding timing was performed to compel arbitration].) In this case, contrary to Respondent San Francisco Unified School District's position, Petitioner did not waive its rights. The CBA provides that the time limits set forth in the grievance and arbitration provision may be extended by mutual agreement. Respondent's letter of September 4, 2018 states that the parties agreed to waive the timing provisions of the CBA. The letter provides: "It should be noted that both sides agreed to waive any timeliness regarding filing and response to the grievance." While Respondent District accuses Petitioner's counsel of lack of candor toward the Court, Respondent omits to mention this explicit agreement by the parties, which is fatal to its waiver argument. The arbitrator shall decide the other merits-based issues that the parties raise, including whether the requested interest is an illegal "penalty."

Petitioner has withdrawn its request for an award of attorneys' fees pursuant to Code of Civil Procedure 128.7. (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript)

Order is signed. (see scanned order for details)

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: M. Goodman, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
